item.title: Pluralistic: How the Epstein Class recruits (20 Jun 2026) 摘要
item.description:
Cory Doctorow discusses the leaked membership data of Dialog, a secret society associated with Peter Thiel, which he describes as a creepy talking-shop for the 'Epstein Class.' He recounts receiving an invitation to speak at a Dialog event, only to realize they expected him to pay thousands of dollars for the privilege. Doctorow invokes Yog's Law ('money flows toward the writer') to explain how such groups prey on creative workers. He concludes that many Dialog members likely fell for this scam, lacking awareness of this principle.
标签: Peter Thiel, secret society, oligarchy, Epstein Class, scam, Yog's Law, creative workers, Dialog, tech culture, power dynamics

item.title: All pieces on a 6 by 5 board 摘要
item.description:
The author uses Claude to generate Z3/Python code solving a chess puzzle: place all pieces (king, queen, two bishops, two knights, two rooks) on a 6×5 board with bishops on opposite colors and no piece attacking another. The Z3 solver finds 192 raw solutions, deduplicated to 24 unique ones by swapping identical piece pairs. The post includes the full Python code using Z3's integer variables and constraints, and displays all 24 solutions with board diagrams and piece coordinates. It also discusses terminology around 'piece' vs 'chessman'.
标签: Z3, Python, chess, puzzle, SMT solver, constraint programming, Claude, AI, code generation, combinatorics
